steps.title: Spot the example pattern
steps.content: The first sentence states a general principle about organisms evolving surprising adaptations; the second sentence then provides a specific organism (tadpole shrimp) that exemplifies this claim. The decisive signal is this movement from general to specific.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”For example,” introduces a specific case illustrating a general principle, which is exactly what tadpole shrimp represent here. “In contrast,” “meanwhile,” and “consequently” express opposition, simultaneity, and causation—relationships that don't apply to providing an illustration. That matches choice B — B is correct.
